what_it_does: A React component that greets the user on return after an absence. It reads a last-visit timestamp from localStorage, fetches /api/greeting scoped to that timestamp, and renders a quiet pill button plus an expandable HUD card. The card auto-opens when the away gap exceeds 4 hours and there is built work or waiting tasks, and can also be opened via a custom open-greeting window event. The card shows current mode/dials, memories that returned on their own, a list of built items, and items waiting on the user.
observations: Uses a module-level `bootedThisLoad` flag to guard against React StrictMode double-running of the non-idempotent localStorage visit write.
Stores `VISIT_KEY` in localStorage and computes `away = Date.now() - last` before writing the new timestamp.
Fetches `/api/greeting` with an optional `?since=` query parameter derived from the stored last-visit time.
Auto-opens the card only when away > AWAY_MS (4 hours) AND there is at least one built or waiting item.
Renders a persistent `● while you were away` pill button that toggles the card.
Listens for a custom window event `open-greeting` as the mobile top-bar entry point.
Displays returned memories with `what_returns`, `condition`, and a `fired_because` tooltip.
Built list supports a show-all toggle when more than six items exist; waiting list simply notes remaining count.
Silently swallows fetch failures and returns null, so no greeting appears if the API is unreachable.
standouts: The returned memories section — a system-initiated memory surface with explicit return conditions.
The away-gap auto-open logic that tries to avoid imposing during a working session.
The module-level StrictMode guard around a non-idempotent localStorage write.
The custom-event hook for a separate mobile chrome entry point.
conventions: Client-side persistence via `localStorage` with a namespaced key constant.
REST fetch with query parameter for server-side scoping.
CSS class vocabulary from a design system (`b`, `ghost`, `hud`, `greet-*`, `muted`).
Inline TypeScript type definitions for API payload shape.
concerns: The localStorage timestamp is written before the fetch, so a failed or interrupted fetch still advances the `since` marker and may lose context for the next visit.
`bootedThisLoad` is module-level and will persist across component remounts and HMR updates, potentially preventing re-fetch when expected.
Auto-open decision ignores `returned_memories` and `surfaced_pending`; a user with only returned memories would not see the card automatically.
No loading or error UI; the greeting simply does not appear while loading or after failure.
Clock skew between client and server could affect the `since` parameter semantics.
notes: This is the morning-arrival greeting surface: it detects absence, pulls overnight context, and surfaces it through a quiet pill and auto-opening HUD.
questions: What server-side logic evaluates the `returned_memories` conditions and produces `fired_because`?
How and where is the `open-greeting` custom event dispatched from the mobile top bar?
What is the full contract of `/api/greeting` beyond the TypeScript shape?
Where is the kit/corpus CSS vocabulary defined and what do the token classes mean?
Should the last-visit marker be scoped per user, tenant, or session rather than a single global key?
Is there a deliberate choice to omit loading and error states, or is that a gap?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a morning greeting that auto-opens after a real away gap and otherwise stays as a quiet pill — matches the implementation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a morning bar moment: a brief, automatic briefing that catches the user up after a genuine absence without interrupting an active work session.
novelty: The combination of a localStorage away-gap detector, an API-scoped `since` parameter, and a dedicated returned-memories surface that frames the system as remembering on its own.
contribution: It contributes the greeting chrome surface, the arrival/absence detection logic, the API fetch scoping, and the rendering structure for returned memories, built work, and waiting tasks.
summary_for_embedding: A React greeting card component that detects user absence via localStorage, fetches a contextual summary from /api/greeting, and renders a toggleable pill and HUD showing returned memories, built items, and waiting tasks.
intention_for_embedding: To greet returning users with a concise, auto-opening morning briefing after a meaningful away gap.

symbols.does: Describes the shape of the greeting data returned by /api/greeting.
symbols.description: Type alias defining fields for `since`, built items/total, waiting items/total, current mode/dials/surfaced pending, and returned memories with condition and explanation.

symbols.does: Renders the greeting pill and expandable HUD card, fetches greeting after away detection, and auto-opens when away exceeds threshold and content exists.
symbols.description: React component that reads the last-visit timestamp from localStorage, fetches `/api/greeting`, conditionally auto-opens the card, and renders sections for returned memories, built work, and waiting tasks; also opens on the custom `open-greeting` event.

symbols.does: Event handler that opens the greeting card.
symbols.description: Local handler registered on the window `open-greeting` event, used as the mobile top-bar entry point to open the greeting HUD.
